title: Clone Graph
problem_statement: Return a deep copy of an undirected graph given a reference node.

examples.output: A deep copy of the graph with the same adjacency relationships.
examples.explanation: Clone each node and reconstruct neighbor references without reusing original nodes.

constraints: The number of nodes is in the range [1, 100].
No duplicate edges or self-loops exist.
intuition: Use BFS or DFS to traverse and clone nodes, mapping original nodes to copies.

approaches.name: DFS clone with hashmap
approaches.description: Recursively clone each neighbor and store the mapping from original to cloned node to avoid duplicates.
approaches.why_not: Iterating without a map can create duplicate clones and invalid references.

interview_qa.q: Why store a map of original to clone?
interview_qa.a: To ensure each original node maps to exactly one clone and to avoid infinite recursion on cycles.
Yes, BFS works equally well by cloning nodes level by level and storing mappings.
